content: The mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell, generating most of the chemical energy.
Cellular respiration occurs in the mitochondria, producing ATP for the cell.
Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert sunlight, water, and CO2 into glucose and oxygen.
Chlorophyll, found in chloroplasts, captures sunlight energy for photosynthesis.
ATP (Adenosine triphosphate) is the primary energy carrier in all living organisms.
The nucleus is a membrane-bound organelle that contains the cell's genetic material (DNA).
Plant cells have a cell wall and chloroplasts, whereas animal cells do not. Both have a nucleus and mitochondria.
The cell membrane regulates the transport of materials entering and exiting the cell.
Ribosomes are macromolecular machines that perform biological protein synthesis (translation).
Gravity is the force by which a planet or other body draws objects toward its center.
The speed of light in a vacuum is exactly 299,792,458 metres per second.
